- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

132	ANALYSIS_SYSTEM = """You are a research evidence analyst. Extract atomic facts from source material.
133	
134	Rules:
135	1. Extract 8-15 atomic facts per source. Focus on the MOST relevant, specific, and unique claims, statistics, findings, and conclusions. Quality over quantity — prioritize facts that DIRECTLY answer the research question over tangential details. Missing a highly relevant fact is worse than including marginally relevant ones.
136	   CRITICAL: ONLY extract from the ARTICLE BODY TEXT. NEVER extract from: navigation menus, cookie consent banners, URL paths/slugs, abbreviation/glossary tables, author byline metadata, page footers/headers, table of contents page numbers, product category labels, or marketing taglines. If the source is mostly non-content (nav, ads, cookie text), extract ZERO facts and set source_quality to 0.0.
137	2. Each atomic fact = ONE specific claim with the EXACT quote from the source (minimum 10 words, up to 200 chars). A quote shorter than 5 words is NEVER acceptable — skip that fact entirely.
138	3. Source quality: Score each source individually on 0.0-1.0 based on:
139	   - Peer review status (peer-reviewed journal > preprint > report > blog)
140	   - Author credentials (named experts > organizations > anonymous)
141	   - Recency (2023-2026 > 2018-2022 > older)
142	   - Specificity to research question (directly relevant > tangentially related)
143	   Do NOT use fixed scores for source types. Vary scores based on each source's specific qualities.
144	4. Rate relevance to the SPECIFIC research question on 0.0-1.0:
145	   - 0.9-1.0: Directly answers part of the research question with specific data
146	   - 0.7-0.8: Provides important context or supporting evidence
147	   - 0.5-0.6: Tangentially relevant or provides background
148	   - 0.1-0.4: Marginally relevant, mostly off-topic
149	   Each fact must get a UNIQUE relevance score. No two facts from the same source should have identical scores.
150	5. Include exact numbers, dates, measurements, names, standards.
151	6. For EVERY quantitative finding, extract ALL of the following when available:
152	   - Effect size with units (MD, SMD, WMD, OR, HR, RR)
153	   - 95% confidence interval (e.g., "95% CI -5.54 to -3.05")
154	   - Heterogeneity (I-squared value, e.g., "I²=96%")
155	   - Number of studies/trials included (e.g., "7 RCTs")
156	   - Total sample size (e.g., "n=269")
157	   - Evidence certainty/quality rating (e.g., "GRADE: moderate", "high certainty")
158	   - Study duration/follow-up period
159	   Missing any of these when the source provides them is a CRITICAL extraction failure.
160	7. For review/meta-analysis papers, extract findings from EACH cited study individually.
161	   Prioritize pooled estimates with I² and CI over narrative summaries.
162	7a. FIX-7 — NO CHERRY-PICKING: When a meta-analysis or systematic review
163	    reports MULTIPLE pooled outcomes, you MUST extract ALL of them, including
164	    non-significant/null results (e.g. "WMD -0.61 kg, 95% CI -1.70 to 0.47;
165	    P = 0.27" for weight loss, alongside the significant "WMD -0.89 µU/mL,
166	    P = 0.009" for fasting insulin). Extracting only the one significant
167	    outcome from a predominantly-null review is a CRITICAL extraction
168	    failure — the downstream synthesizer will present a misleading headline.
169	    When a source reports outcomes for weight, glucose, HbA1c, triglycerides,
170	    and insulin and only insulin was significant, extract facts for ALL FIVE
171	    outcomes so synthesis can report the complete picture.
172	7b. FIX-7 — RISKS, ADVERSE EVENTS, SAFETY: When the source discusses risks,
173	    harms, side-effects, adverse events, contraindications, toxicity, or
174	    safety concerns, you MUST extract at least one atomic fact per distinct
175	    risk category. Examples of risk content that must be captured when
176	    present: dizziness/nausea/headache/gastrointestinal symptoms, bone
177	    density or lean-mass loss, hypoglycemia risk, psychological symptoms
178	    (irritability, difficulty concentrating), population-specific cautions
179	    (pregnancy, diabetes, eating disorders), interactions, long-term unknowns,
180	    or explicit statements that side-effects were not systematically
181	    quantified. Tag these with perspective="Public_Health" or
182	    perspective="Scientific" and fact_category="risk" or
183	    fact_category="adverse_event". Missing a documented adverse event when
184	    the source reports it is a CRITICAL extraction failure.
185	8. For each atomic fact, tag the research perspective it belongs to.
186	9. CRITICAL (ARCH-4): The 'statement' field must be a DIRECT PARAPHRASE of the source text.
187	   Do NOT infer, extrapolate, or add information beyond what the direct_quote states.
188	   If the direct_quote is fewer than 10 words, the statement must be equally narrow in scope.
189	10. QUOTE LENGTH: The 'direct_quote' field MUST be 150 characters or fewer.
190	   Quote only the KEY phrase, not entire sentences or paragraphs.
191	   This forces you to identify the most important fragment of evidence.
192	   BAD: "A systematic review and meta-analysis of 24 randomized controlled trials totaling 1,768 participants found that alternate-day fasting produced weight loss comparable to continuous energy restriction"
193	   GOOD: "alternate-day fasting produced weight loss comparable to continuous energy restriction"
194	   A 2-word quote like 'distilled water' can only support a statement about distilled water,
195	   not a full sentence about healthcare settings or bacterial contamination.
196	   Perspectives: Scientific, Regulatory, Industry, Economic, Public_Health, Historical, Regional, Methodological, Emerging_Trends.
197	   Choose the single best-fit perspective.
198	
199	Output format (return ONLY this JSON structure):
200	{"analyses": [{"source_url": "https://example.com", "source_title": "Study Title", "source_type": "journal_article", "source_quality": 0.8, "overall_relevance": 0.7, "year": 2024, "authors": ["Smith J"], "venue": "Water Research", "doi": "", "atomic_facts": [{"statement": "E. coli was detected in 30% of tested filters after 6 months", "direct_quote": "E. coli presence in 30% of tested POU filters", "fact_category": "statistic", "relevance_score": 0.9, "confidence": 0.8, "perspective": "Scientific"}], "evidence_summary": "Bacterial contamination in POU water filters"}]}"""
201	
202	
203	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
204	# Pre-fetch authority gate (replaced FIX-B1 blocklist 2026-04-12)
205	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
206	
207	# Domain blocklist removed 2026-04-12 — replaced by PageRank/tier authority
208	# gate (mirrors commit 74e1bf6 which removed it from the wiki path). Commerce
209	# domains formerly in blocked_domains are now in low_credibility_domains
210	# (authority 0.2). The pre-fetch authority gate (PG_AUTHORITY_GATE, default
211	# 0.3 in searcher.py) and the analyzer's _get_domain_authority() do the
212	# filtering. Hard blocklists don't scale (infinite tail of garbage domains)
213	# and were the source of false negatives that limited source diversity.
214	#
215	# Authority gate threshold for pre-fetch filtering. Sources scoring below
216	# this are skipped before fetch. Set permissively (0.3) — synthesis stage
217	# applies stricter gates downstream.
218	_AUTHORITY_GATE_PREFETCH = float(os.getenv("PG_AUTHORITY_GATE", "0.3"))
219	
220	
221	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
222	# FIX-B2: Domain authority scoring
223	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
224	
225	# LAW VI: All domain tier lists loaded from config/settings/domain_lists.yaml
226	_TIER1_TLDS = frozenset([".gov", ".edu"])  # Universal TLDs, not domain-specific
227	
228	# TIER 2 partial matches (universal academic infrastructure)
229	_TIER2_PARTIALS = frozenset(["pubmed", "pmc"])
230	
231	# TIER 2 path-qualified (universal news outlets)
232	_TIER2_DOMAIN_PATHS = [("bbc.com", "/news")]
233	
234	_DOMAIN_AUTHORITY_TIER1 = 1.0
235	_DOMAIN_AUTHORITY_TIER2 = 0.85
236	_DOMAIN_AUTHORITY_TIER3 = 0.7
237	_DOMAIN_AUTHORITY_DEFAULT = float(os.getenv("PG_DEFAULT_DOMAIN_AUTHORITY", "0.5"))
238	
239	
240	def _get_domain_authority(url: str) -> float:
241	    """FIX-B2: Return a domain authority multiplier for the given URL.
242	
243	    Tiers:
244	    - TIER 1 (1.0):  .gov, .edu, top journals (Nature, Science, etc.)
245	    - TIER 2 (0.85): PubMed, NIH, standards bodies, wire services
246	    - TIER 3 (0.7):  Industry trade publications (non-blog)
247	    - TIER 4 (PG_DEFAULT_DOMAIN_AUTHORITY, default 0.5): Everything else
248	    - LOW (PG_LOW_CREDIBILITY_AUTHORITY, default 0.2): Consumer health,
249	      commerce sites, popular media that simplify research.
250	
251	    The multiplier is applied to relevance_score before tier assignment,
252	    so commercial blogs get lower tiers. The pre-fetch authority gate
253	    (PG_AUTHORITY_GATE, default 0.3) drops anything below the threshold
254	    before fetch — this replaces the old hard blocklist (removed 2026-04-12).
255	    """
256	    if not url:
257	        return _DOMAIN_AUTHORITY_DEFAULT
258	
259	    url_lower = url.lower()
260	    try:
261	        parsed = urlparse(url_lower)
262	        hostname = parsed.hostname or ""
263	        path = parsed.path or ""
264	    except Exception:
265	        return _DOMAIN_AUTHORITY_DEFAULT
266	
267	    # TIER 1: Check TLDs (.gov, .edu) with path-based demotion.
268	    # FIX-B4: .edu TLD gave blanket 1.0 to community college blogs,
269	    # university news pages, and student projects — same as Nature/BMJ.
270	    # Now: .edu gets TIER 1 only for journal/research paths. News,
271	    # blogs, and general pages get TIER 2 (0.85).
272	    # FIX-B4: Demote .edu subdomains/paths that are news, blogs, etc.
273	    # FIX-B4: .edu demotion — two levels based on content type
274	    _EDU_BLOG_PATTERNS = {"blog", "myctcd", "wordpress", "sites/"}
275	    _EDU_NEWS_PATTERNS = {"news", "today", "stories", "press", "media", "magazine", "events"}
276	    for tld in _TIER1_TLDS:
277	        if hostname.endswith(tld):
278	            if tld == ".edu":
279	                _host_parts = hostname.replace(tld, "").split(".")
280	                _combined = path + " " + " ".join(_host_parts)
281	                # Blog/student content → LOW credibility (below gate threshold)
282	                if any(bp in _combined for bp in _EDU_BLOG_PATTERNS):
283	                    return _DOMAIN_AUTHORITY_DEFAULT  # 0.5 — below B3 gate 0.6
284	                # Institutional news → TIER 2 (above gate, not TIER 1)
285	                if any(np in _combined for np in _EDU_NEWS_PATTERNS):
286	                    return _DOMAIN_AUTHORITY_TIER2  # 0.85
287	            return _DOMAIN_AUTHORITY_TIER1
288	
289	    # LAW VI: All domain lists from config (not hardcoded)
290	    _cfg = _get_domain_config()
291	
292	    # TIER 1: Check specific domains (exact or subdomain match)
293	    for domain in _cfg.tier1_domains:
294	        if hostname == domain or hostname.endswith("." + domain):
295	            return _DOMAIN_AUTHORITY_TIER1
296	
297	    # TIER 2: Check specific domains
298	    for domain in _cfg.tier2_domains:
299	        if hostname == domain or hostname.endswith("." + domain):
300	            return _DOMAIN_AUTHORITY_TIER2
301	
302	    # TIER 2: Check partial hostname matches (pubmed, pmc)
303	    for partial in _TIER2_PARTIALS:
304	        if partial in hostname:
305	            return _DOMAIN_AUTHORITY_TIER2
306	
307	    # TIER 2: Path-qualified (e.g., bbc.com/news)
308	    for domain, required_path in _TIER2_DOMAIN_PATHS:
309	        if (hostname == domain or hostname.endswith("." + domain)):
310	            if required_path in path:
311	                return _DOMAIN_AUTHORITY_TIER2
312	
313	    # TIER 3: Trade/industry publications (domain-specific, from config)
314	    for domain in _cfg.tier3_domains:
315	        if hostname == domain or hostname.endswith("." + domain):
316	            if "/blog" in path:

342	# FIX-S2-4: Source-level topic gate
343	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
344	
345	
346	async def _source_topic_gate(
347	    fetched: list[dict],
348	    query: str,
349	    timeout_seconds: int = 60,
350	) -> list[dict]:
351	    """FIX-S2-4: Filter entire sources by embedding similarity BEFORE evidence extraction.
352	
353	    Semantic Scholar returns wildly off-topic papers (tick genetics, bat fungus,
354	    psychiatry) that contaminate the evidence pool. This gate computes cosine
355	    similarity between the research query and each source's title+abstract/snippet,
356	    removing sources below PG_SOURCE_TOPIC_GATE threshold.
357	
358	    This is a SOURCE-level filter (coarse, fast, runs once on titles) as opposed
359	    to the existing FIX-B3 EVIDENCE-level off-topic filter (fine-grained, runs
360	    on extracted statements after LLM analysis). Filtering here avoids wasting
361	    LLM calls on sources that are clearly irrelevant.
362	
363	    Runs embedding in a thread pool with asyncio timeout to prevent event-loop
364	    starvation from CPU-bound sentence-transformers.
365	
366	    Graceful fallback: if embedding fails or times out, returns fetched unchanged.
367	
368	    Args:
369	        fetched: List of fetched source dicts (url, title, content, snippet, ...).
370	        query: The original research query.
371	        timeout_seconds: Max seconds for the embedding operation.
372	
373	    Returns:
374	        Filtered list of fetched source dicts.
375	    """
376	    if not fetched:
377	        return fetched
378	
379	    threshold = _PG_SOURCE_TOPIC_GATE
380	
381	    # Build source-level text: title + abstract/snippet for each source.
382	    # Content may be very long (10K+ chars); title+abstract is sufficient for
383	    # topic relevance and keeps embedding fast.
384	    source_texts = []
385	    for src in fetched:
386	        title = src.get("title", "")
387	        # Prefer snippet (usually abstract for academic sources) over full content
388	        abstract_or_snippet = src.get("snippet", "")
389	        if not abstract_or_snippet:
390	            # Fall back to first 500 chars of content as abstract proxy
391	            abstract_or_snippet = src.get("content", "")[:500]
392	        source_text = (title + ". " + abstract_or_snippet).strip()
393	        # If somehow empty, use a placeholder that will score low
394	        if not source_text or source_text == ".":
395	            source_text = title if title else "unknown source"
396	        source_texts.append(source_text)
397	
398	    def _do_source_embedding():
399	        """Synchronous embedding for source-level topic gate, runs in thread pool."""
400	        from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_text, embed_texts
401	
402	        t0 = time.time()
403	
404	        # Embed query once
405	        query_vec = np.array(embed_text(query))
406	
407	        # Batch-embed all source texts
408	        source_vecs = np.array(embed_texts(source_texts))
409	
410	        # Cosine similarity (embeddings are pre-normalized -> dot product)
411	        similarities = source_vecs @ query_vec
412	
413	        elapsed = time.time() - t0
414	        logger.info(
415	            "[polaris graph] FIX-S2-4: Source embedding complete in %.1fs for %d sources. "
416	            "sim min=%.3f, median=%.3f, max=%.3f",
417	            elapsed,
418	            len(fetched),
419	            float(np.min(similarities)),
420	            float(np.median(similarities)),
421	            float(np.max(similarities)),
422	        )
423	        return similarities
424	
425	    try:
426	        logger.info(
427	            "[polaris graph] FIX-S2-4: Running source topic gate on %d sources "
428	            "(threshold=%.2f, timeout=%ds)...",
429	            len(fetched),
430	            threshold,
431	            timeout_seconds,
432	        )
433	
434	        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
435	        similarities = await asyncio.wait_for(
436	            loop.run_in_executor(None, _do_source_embedding),
437	            timeout=timeout_seconds,
438	        )
439	
440	        # Filter sources below threshold
441	        before_count = len(fetched)
442	        passed = []
443	        removed_details = []
444	        for i, src in enumerate(fetched):
445	            sim = float(similarities[i])
446	            if sim >= threshold:
447	                passed.append(src)
448	            else:
449	                removed_details.append(
450	                    (src.get("title", "?")[:80], sim)
451	                )
452	
453	        removed_count = before_count - len(passed)
454	
455	        if removed_count > 0:
456	            logger.info(
457	                "[polaris graph] FIX-S2-4: Source topic gate removed %d/%d sources "
458	                "with similarity < %.2f",
459	                removed_count,
460	                before_count,
461	                threshold,
462	            )
463	            # Log removed source titles at DEBUG level for diagnosis
464	            for removed_title, removed_sim in removed_details[:10]:
465	                logger.debug(
466	                    "[polaris graph] FIX-S2-4: Removed source: '%s' (sim=%.3f)",
467	                    removed_title,
468	                    removed_sim,
469	                )
470	            if len(removed_details) > 10:
471	                logger.debug(
472	                    "[polaris graph] FIX-S2-4: ... and %d more removed sources",
473	                    len(removed_details) - 10,
474	                )
475	
476	            # OBS-4: Trace source-level filtering
477	            tracer = get_tracer()
478	            if tracer:
479	                tracer.evidence(
480	                    "analyze", "source_topic_gated", len(passed),
481	                    removed=removed_count,
482	                    threshold=threshold,
483	                )
484	        else:
485	            logger.info(
486	                "[polaris graph] FIX-S2-4: Source topic gate passed all %d sources "
487	                "(threshold=%.2f)",
488	                before_count,
489	                threshold,
490	            )
491	
492	        return passed
493	
494	    except (asyncio.TimeoutError, ImportError, Exception) as exc:
495	        # FIX-TOPIC-FALLBACK: Keyword-based fallback when embeddings unavailable.
496	        # Without this, ALL sources pass through (including S2 noise like tick
497	        # genetics for water filtration queries). Extract query keywords and
498	        # keep sources whose title/snippet contains at least one keyword.
499	        logger.warning(
500	            "[polaris graph] FIX-S2-4: Source topic gate embedding failed: %s — "
501	            "applying keyword fallback on %d sources",
502	            str(exc)[:200],
503	            len(fetched),
504	        )
505	        query_keywords = {
506	            w.lower().strip(".,;:!?()[]\"'")
507	            for w in query.split()
508	            if len(w.strip(".,;:!?()[]\"'")) > 3
509	        }
510	        if not query_keywords:
511	            return fetched
512	
513	        passed = []
514	        for src, src_text in zip(fetched, source_texts):
515	            text_lower = src_text.lower()
516	            hits = sum(1 for kw in query_keywords if kw in text_lower)
517	            if hits >= 1:
518	                passed.append(src)
519	        if len(passed) < max(5, len(fetched) // 3):
520	            # Fallback too aggressive — keep all to avoid starving pipeline
521	            logger.warning(
522	                "[polaris graph] FIX-TOPIC-FALLBACK: Keyword filter too aggressive "
523	                "(%d/%d passed) — keeping all sources",
524	                len(passed), len(fetched),
525	            )
526	            return fetched
527	        logger.info(
528	            "[polaris graph] FIX-TOPIC-FALLBACK: Keyword fallback kept %d/%d sources "
529	            "(%d removed for zero query keyword overlap)",
530	            len(passed), len(fetched), len(fetched) - len(passed),
531	        )
532	        return passed
533

539	_STRUCTURED_DATA_SYSTEM = (
540	    "You are a structured data extraction specialist. "
541	    "Extract all numerical data points from the provided text. "
542	    "For each data point: identify the data_type "
543	    "(statistic/comparison/time_series/measurement/ranking), "
544	    "label, value, year, unit, and context."
545	)

547	
548	async def _extract_structured_data(
549	    client: OpenRouterClient,
550	    content: str,
551	    source_url: str,
552	    evidence_id: str,
553	) -> list[dict]:
554	    """Extract structured data points (statistics, comparisons, time-series) from source content.
555	
556	    Returns list of StructuredDataPoint dicts.
557	
558	    Gated behind PG_STRUCTURED_DATA_EXTRACTION env var (default '0' = disabled).
559	    Uses generate_structured() with StructuredDataExtraction schema.
560	    On error, returns empty list (logs warning).
561	    """
562	    if not content or not content.strip():
563	        return []
564	
565	    # Truncate to ~5000 chars to save tokens (LAW VI: cap from constant, not magic number)
566	    truncated_content = content[:5000]
567	
568	    prompt = (
569	        f"Extract all numerical data points from this text. "
570	        f"For each: identify the data_type (statistic/comparison/time_series/"
571	        f"measurement/ranking), label, value, year, unit, and context.\n\n"
572	        f"Source URL: {source_url}\n"
573	        f"Content:\n{truncated_content}"
574	    )
575	
576	    # FIX-TIMEOUT: Structured data extraction timeout configurable via env var.
577	    # LLM takes 200+ seconds on complex content; previous 60s caused 100% timeouts.
578	    _sd_timeout = int(os.getenv("PG_STRUCTURED_DATA_TIMEOUT", "300"))
579	
580	    try:
581	        parsed = await client.generate_structured(
582	            prompt=prompt,
583	            schema=StructuredDataExtraction,
584	            system=_STRUCTURED_DATA_SYSTEM,
585	            max_tokens=int(os.getenv("PG_STRUCTURED_DATA_MAX_TOKENS", "4096")),
586	            timeout=_sd_timeout,
587	            reasoning_enabled=False,
588	        )
589	
590	        results = []
591	        for dp in parsed.data_points:
592	            point_dict = dp.model_dump()
593	            point_dict["evidence_id"] = evidence_id
594	            point_dict["source_url"] = source_url
595	            results.append(point_dict)
596	
597	        if results:
598	            logger.info(
599	                "[polaris graph] GEMINI-ARCH: Extracted %d structured data points "
600	                "from %s",
601	                len(results),
602	                source_url[:80],
603	            )
604	        return results
605	
606	    except Exception as exc:
607	        logger.warning(
608	            "[polaris graph] GEMINI-ARCH: Structured data extraction failed "
609	            "for %s: %s — returning empty list",
610	            source_url[:80],
611	            str(exc)[:200],
612	        )
613	        return []

616	async def _enrich_evidence_cards(
617	    client: OpenRouterClient,
618	    evidence: list[dict],
619	    source_contents: dict[str, str],
620	) -> list[dict]:
621	    """RC-1: Post-extraction enrichment -- add methodology, conditions, limitations, comparable metrics.
622	
623	    Processes evidence in batches of 10 per LLM call for cost efficiency.
624	    Merges enrichment fields back into evidence dicts in-place.
625	
626	    Args:
627	        client: OpenRouter LLM client.
628	        evidence: List of EvidencePiece dicts to enrich.
629	        source_contents: {source_url: content_text} for context.
630	
631	    Returns:
632	        The same evidence list with enrichment fields populated.
633	    """
634	    if not evidence:
635	        return evidence
636	
637	    from src.polaris_graph.schemas import EvidenceCardBatch
638	
639	    batch_size = int(os.getenv("PG_V3_CARD_BATCH_SIZE", "10"))
640	    enriched_count = 0
641	
642	    for batch_start in range(0, len(evidence), batch_size):
643	        batch = evidence[batch_start:batch_start + batch_size]
644	
645	        # Build batch prompt
646	        evidence_lines = []
647	        for ev in batch:
648	            url = ev.get("source_url", "")
649	            content_snippet = source_contents.get(url, "")[:2000]
650	            evidence_lines.append(
651	                f"Evidence ID: {ev.get('evidence_id', '?')}\n"
652	                f"Statement: {ev.get('statement', '')}\n"
653	                f"Quote: {ev.get('direct_quote', '')[:300]}\n"
654	                f"Source context: {content_snippet[:500]}"
655	            )
656	
657	        batch_text = "\n---\n".join(evidence_lines)
658	
659	        system = (
660	            "For each evidence finding below, extract:\n"
661	            "1. methodology: How this finding was obtained (experimental method, study type)\n"
662	            "2. conditions: Key experimental parameters (temperature, pH, concentration, sample size)\n"
663	            "3. limitations: Any stated limitations of this finding\n"
664	            "4. strength_signals: Quality indicators from this list ONLY: "
665	            "peer_reviewed, large_sample, replicated, meta_analysis, rct, longitudinal\n"
666	            "5. comparable_metrics: Quantitative values that could be compared across studies.\n"
667	            "   Each metric needs: metric_name, value (number), unit, condition, entity\n\n"
668	            "Return a JSON object with a 'cards' array containing one enrichment per evidence piece.\n"
669	            "If information is not available, use empty strings/lists. Do NOT invent data."
670	        )
671	
672	        try:
673	            result = await client.generate_structured(
674	                prompt=f"Enrich these {len(batch)} evidence pieces:\n\n{batch_text}",
675	                schema=EvidenceCardBatch,
676	                system=system,
677	                max_tokens=int(os.getenv("PG_STRUCTURED_DATA_MAX_TOKENS", "4096")),
678	                timeout=int(os.getenv("PG_V3_CARD_TIMEOUT", "120")),
679	            )
680	
681	            # Merge enrichments back into evidence dicts
682	            card_map = {c.evidence_id: c for c in result.cards}
683	            for ev in batch:
684	                card = card_map.get(ev.get("evidence_id", ""))
685	                if card:
686	                    ev["methodology"] = card.methodology or ev.get("methodology")
687	                    ev["conditions"] = card.conditions or ev.get("conditions")
688	                    ev["limitations"] = card.limitations or ev.get("limitations")
689	                    ev["strength_signals"] = card.strength_signals or ev.get("strength_signals")
690	                    if card.comparable_metrics:
691	                        ev["comparable_metrics"] = [
692	                            m.model_dump() for m in card.comparable_metrics
693	                        ]
694	                    enriched_count += 1
695	        except Exception as exc:
696	            logger.warning(
697	                "[polaris graph] RC-1: Evidence card enrichment failed for batch %d-%d: %s",
698	                batch_start, batch_start + len(batch), str(exc)[:200],
699	            )
700

700	
701	    logger.info(
702	        "[polaris graph] RC-1: Enriched %d/%d evidence pieces with cards",
703	        enriched_count, len(evidence),
704	    )
705	    return evidence

708	async def analyze_sources(
709	    client: OpenRouterClient,
710	    state: ResearchState,
711	    on_evidence_progress: "callable | None" = None,
712	) -> dict:
713	    """
714	    Fetch and analyze all search results.
715	
716	    Returns state update with evidence list.
717	
718	    Args:
719	        on_evidence_progress: Optional callback called after each batch completes
720	            with the current accumulated evidence list. Used by graph.py to
721	            update the timeout-recovery snapshot progressively.
722	    """
723	    web_results = state.get("web_results", [])
724	    academic_results = state.get("academic_results", [])
725	    query = state["original_query"]
726	
727	    # Merge and rank all results
728	    all_results = _rank_and_merge(web_results, academic_results)
729	
730	    # Cap at max sources to analyze
731	    sources_to_analyze = all_results[:MAX_SOURCES_TO_ANALYZE]
732	
733	    # FIX-SNIPPET-RERANK: Pre-fetch snippet reranking by embedding similarity.
734	    # Drops bottom PG_SNIPPET_DROP_PCT of results before costly content fetching.
735	    # FIX-C4: Use module-level constants instead of inline env var reads
736	    snippet_drop_pct = PG_SNIPPET_DROP_PCT
737	    snippet_rerank_enabled = PG_SNIPPET_RERANK_ENABLED
738	    if snippet_rerank_enabled and len(sources_to_analyze) >= 10:
739	        try:
740	            from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_texts as _embed_texts
741	            snippets = [
742	                (r.get("snippet") or r.get("title") or "")[:500]
743	                for r in sources_to_analyze
744	            ]
745	            # Filter out empty snippets
746	            non_empty = [(i, s) for i, s in enumerate(snippets) if s.strip()]
747	            if non_empty and len(non_empty) >= 5:
748	                import numpy as _np
749	                snippet_texts = [s for _, s in non_empty]
750	                all_texts = [query] + snippet_texts
751	                embeddings = _np.array(_embed_texts(all_texts), dtype=_np.float32)
752	                query_emb = embeddings[0:1]  # (1, d)
753	                snippet_embs = embeddings[1:]  # (n, d)
754	                # Cosine similarity (embeddings are L2-normalized)
755	                sims = (snippet_embs @ query_emb.T).flatten()
756	                # Map back to original indices with scores
757	                scored = [(non_empty[j][0], float(sims[j])) for j in range(len(non_empty))]
758	                scored.sort(key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
759	                # Keep top (1 - drop_pct) of results
760	                keep_count = max(5, int(len(scored) * (1 - snippet_drop_pct)))
761	                keep_indices = set(idx for idx, _ in scored[:keep_count])
762	                # Also keep results that had empty snippets (can't score them)
763	                empty_indices = set(range(len(sources_to_analyze))) - set(
764	                    i for i, _ in non_empty
765	                )
766	                keep_indices.update(empty_indices)
767	                pre_count = len(sources_to_analyze)
768	                sources_to_analyze = [
769	                    r for i, r in enumerate(sources_to_analyze) if i in keep_indices
770	                ]
771	                dropped = pre_count - len(sources_to_analyze)
772	                if dropped > 0:
773	                    logger.info(
774	                        "[polaris graph] FIX-SNIPPET-RERANK: Dropped %d/%d sources "
775	                        "below query similarity threshold (kept %d, drop_pct=%.0f%%)",
776	                        dropped, pre_count, len(sources_to_analyze),
777	                        snippet_drop_pct * 100,
778	                    )
779	                    # OBS-TRACE: Emission 6 — Snippet rerank
780	                    tracer = get_tracer()
781	                    if tracer:
782	                        tracer.evidence("analyze", "snippet_reranked", len(sources_to_analyze),
783	                            reranked_count=len(sources_to_analyze),
784	                            dropped=dropped, pre_count=pre_count)
785	        except Exception as exc:
786	            logger.warning(
787	                "[polaris graph] FIX-SNIPPET-RERANK: Failed (non-fatal): %s",
788	                str(exc)[:200],
789	            )
790	
791	    logger.info(
792	        "[polaris graph] Analyzing %d sources (from %d total)",
793	        len(sources_to_analyze),
794	        len(all_results),
795	    )
796	
797	    # Fetch content for all sources (concurrency from env)
798	    fetched, content_cache_hits = await _fetch_all_content(
799	        sources_to_analyze, concurrency=PG_FETCH_CONCURRENCY
800	    )
801	
802	    # FIX-S2-4: Source-level topic gate — filter entire sources by embedding
803	    # similarity BEFORE evidence extraction. Prevents wasting LLM calls on
804	    # off-topic S2 papers (tick genetics, bat fungus, psychiatry, oncology).
805	    fetched = await _source_topic_gate(fetched, query)
806	
807	    # RC-4: Content quality gate (v3 Hybrid) — reject garbled/boilerplate before extraction
808	    if os.getenv("PG_V3_CONTENT_QUALITY_GATE", "0") == "1":
809	        from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.content_quality_gate import score_content_quality
810	        threshold = float(os.getenv("PG_V3_CONTENT_QUALITY_THRESHOLD", "0.3"))
811	        pre_gate_count = len(fetched)
812	        quality_passed = []
813	        for item in fetched:
814	            content_text = item.get("content", "")
815	            url = item.get("url", "")
816	            if not content_text:
817	                quality_passed.append(item)  # Can't score empty, let downstream handle
818	                continue
819	            quality_score, reasons = score_content_quality(content_text, url)
820	            if quality_score >= threshold:
821	                quality_passed.append(item)
822	            else:
823	                logger.warning(
824	                    "[polaris graph] RC-4: Rejecting %s (quality=%.2f, reasons=%s)",
825	                    url[:80], quality_score, reasons,
826	                )
827	        fetched = quality_passed
828	        rejected = pre_gate_count - len(fetched)
829	        if rejected > 0:
830	            logger.info(
831	                "[polaris graph] RC-4: Content quality gate rejected %d/%d sources",
832	                rejected, pre_gate_count,
833	            )
834	
835	
836	    # Batch size from env (LAW VI). Default 1 = one source per LLM call.
837	    # Concurrency from env (PG_ANALYSIS_CONCURRENCY).
838	    evidence: list[EvidencePiece] = []
839	    batch_size = PG_ANALYSIS_BATCH_SIZE
840	    semaphore = asyncio.Semaphore(PG_ANALYSIS_CONCURRENCY)
841	    # Per-batch timeout from env — prevents single slow batches from blocking
842	    batch_timeout = PG_ANALYSIS_BATCH_TIMEOUT
843	
844	    batches = [
845	        fetched[i : i + batch_size]
846	        for i in range(0, len(fetched), batch_size)
847	    ]
848	
849	    completed_count = 0
850	
851	    evidence_cap = PG_MAX_EVIDENCE_TO_EXTRACT
852	    budget_exhausted = False
853	
854	    async def _run_batch(batch_idx: int, batch: list[dict]) -> list[EvidencePiece]:
855	        nonlocal completed_count, budget_exhausted
856	        # FIX-BUDGET: Skip batch if evidence cap already reached
857	        if budget_exhausted:
858	            return []
859	        async with semaphore:
860	            try:
861	                result = await asyncio.wait_for(
862	                    _analyze_batch(client, batch, query),
863	                    timeout=batch_timeout,
864	                )
865	            except asyncio.TimeoutError:
866	                logger.warning(
867	                    "[polaris graph] Batch %d/%d timed out after %.0fs — retrying once with same batch",
868	                    batch_idx + 1, len(batches), batch_timeout,
869	                )
870	                # FIX-A1: Retry once on timeout before giving up
871	                try:
872	                    result = await asyncio.wait_for(
873	                        _analyze_batch(client, batch, query),
874	                        timeout=batch_timeout,
875	                    )
876	                except (asyncio.TimeoutError, Exception) as retry_exc:
877	                    logger.error(
878	                        "[polaris graph] FIX-A1: Batch %d/%d retry also failed: %s",
879	                        batch_idx + 1, len(batches), str(retry_exc)[:100],
880	                    )
881	                    return []
882	            if result:
883	                # Progressive accumulation — evidence available immediately
884	                evidence.extend(result)
885	                completed_count += 1
886	                logger.info(
887	                    "[polaris graph] Batch %d/%d: extracted %d evidence pieces "
888	                    "(%d total so far)",
889	                    batch_idx + 1,
890	                    len(batches),
891	                    len(result),
892	                    len(evidence),

1863	def _clean_content(raw: str, url: str, extract_fn) -> str:
1864	    """Clean raw content: lightweight HTML stripping for direct-fetch HTML.
1865	
1866	    FIX-QM25: Does NOT use trafilatura (CPU-bound, blocks event loop via GIL).
1867	    Jina Reader and Firecrawl already return clean markdown. For the rare
1868	    direct-fetch fallback that returns HTML, uses fast regex stripping.
1869	    """
1870	    import re as _re
1871	
1872	    # Cap content to prevent memory issues
1873	    capped = raw[:100_000]
1874	
1875	    # Detect HTML by checking for common HTML markers
1876	    lower_start = capped[:500].lower()
1877	    if "<html" in lower_start or "<body" in lower_start or "<!doctype" in lower_start:
1878	        # Lightweight HTML-to-text: strip tags, decode entities, collapse whitespace
1879	        text = _re.sub(r"<script[^>]*>.*?</script>", " ", capped, flags=_re.DOTALL | _re.IGNORECASE)
1880	        text = _re.sub(r"<style[^>]*>.*?</style>", " ", text, flags=_re.DOTALL | _re.IGNORECASE)
1881	        text = _re.sub(r"<[^>]+>", " ", text)
1882	        text = _re.sub(r" ", " ", text)
1883	        text = _re.sub(r"&", "&", text)
1884	        text = _re.sub(r"<", "<", text)
1885	        text = _re.sub(r">", ">", text)
1886	        text = _re.sub(r"&#\d+;", " ", text)
1887	        text = _re.sub(r"\s+", " ", text).strip()
1888	        if len(text) >= 100:
1889	            return text
1890	
1891	    return capped

1894	def _strip_non_article_content(content: str) -> str:
1895	    """FIX-047-K3: Remove non-article content before LLM extraction.
1896	
1897	    T047 audit found 24 evidence pieces (9.3%) extracted from cookie consent
1898	    banners, navigation menus, page footers, and other non-article elements.
1899	    This function strips common non-content patterns from fetched text.
1900	
1901	    Does NOT use trafilatura (CPU-bound, blocks event loop per FIX-QM25).
1902	    Uses fast regex heuristics instead.
1903	    """
1904	    import re as _re
1905	
1906	    if not content or len(content) < 100:
1907	        return content
1908	
1909	    lines = content.split("\n")
1910	    filtered_lines = []
1911	    for line in lines:
1912	        stripped = line.strip()
1913	        if not stripped:
1914	            filtered_lines.append(line)
1915	            continue
1916	
1917	        lower = stripped.lower()
1918	
1919	        # Skip cookie consent / privacy banner lines
1920	        if any(phrase in lower for phrase in (
1921	            "cookie", "we use cookies", "privacy policy", "accept all",
1922	            "reject all", "cookie settings", "consent to", "advertising",
1923	            "track the user", "manage preferences", "gdpr",
1924	        )):
1925	            continue
1926	
1927	        # Skip navigation-style lines (short, title case, no punctuation)
1928	        if (
1929	            len(stripped) < 40
1930	            and stripped.istitle()
1931	            and not any(c in stripped for c in ".?!:;,")
1932	            and not any(c.isdigit() for c in stripped)
1933	        ):
1934	            continue
1935	
1936	        # Skip lines that are mostly URL paths
1937	        url_char_count = sum(1 for c in stripped if c in "/:?&=.#%")
1938	        if len(stripped) > 10 and url_char_count / len(stripped) > 0.3:
1939	            continue
1940	
1941	        # Skip social media sharing buttons and copyright lines
1942	        if any(phrase in lower for phrase in (
1943	            "share on facebook", "share on twitter", "share on linkedin",
1944	            "follow us", "subscribe to", "all rights reserved", "copyright ©",
1945	        )):
1946	            continue
1947	
1948	        filtered_lines.append(line)
1949	
1950	    result = "\n".join(filtered_lines)
1951	
1952	    # Collapse excessive whitespace
1953	    result = _re.sub(r"\n{3,}", "\n\n", result)
1954	
1955	    return result.strip()

1996	def _is_stub_content(content: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
1997	    """Return (is_stub, reason). True when content is a captcha/access-block
1998	    page or so short/generic it cannot carry research evidence.
1999	
2000	    False-positive guard: weak markers (e.g., 'cloudflare', 'sci-hub') require
2001	    2+ co-occurrences in <1000-char content. Strong markers (specific bot-
2002	    challenge phrasing) flag on single occurrence in <2000-char content.
2003	    """
2004	    if not content:
2005	        return True, "empty"
2006	    lc = content.lower()
2007	    # Strong-marker path: single specific bot-challenge phrase in short doc.
2008	    if len(content) < 2000:
2009	        for m in _STUB_CONTENT_STRONG_MARKERS:
2010	            if m in lc:
2011	                return True, f"strong-marker='{m}' in short content ({len(content)} chars)"
2012	    # Weak-marker path: require 2+ co-occurrences and tighter length gate
2013	    # so legitimate short-abstract academic pages aren't accidentally dropped.
2014	    if len(content) < 1000:
2015	        weak_hits = [m for m in _STUB_CONTENT_WEAK_MARKERS if m in lc]
2016	        if len(weak_hits) >= 2:
2017	            return True, f"weak-markers={weak_hits} in short content ({len(content)} chars)"
2018	    # Very short with only punctuation / IP / boilerplate
2019	    if len(content) < 400:
2020	        alpha_ratio = sum(1 for c in content if c.isalpha()) / max(1, len(content))
2021	        if alpha_ratio < 0.55:
2022	            return True, f"low-alpha boilerplate ({alpha_ratio:.2f} alpha ratio, {len(content)} chars)"
2023	    return False, ""

2297	def _rank_and_merge(
2298	    web_results: list[dict],
2299	    academic_results: list[dict],
2300	) -> list[dict]:
2301	    """Merge web and academic results with balanced representation.
2302	
2303	    Previous approach gave academic a 2x boost, causing 7K+ noisy S2
2304	    papers to drown out 500+ relevant web results.  Now we interleave:
2305	    take top web results first (they're almost always on-topic from
2306	    Serper), then top academic results.
2307	    """
2308	    # Sort each pool by its own relevance
2309	    web_sorted = sorted(
2310	        web_results,
2311	        key=lambda r: r.get("score", 0.5),
2312	        reverse=True,
2313	    )
2314	    # FIX-059-E (H-11): S2 papers without scores default to 0.0, not 0.5.
2315	    # This prevents unscored papers from ranking above genuinely relevant results.
2316	    academic_sorted = sorted(
2317	        academic_results,
2318	        key=lambda r: r.get("score", 0.0),
2319	        reverse=True,
2320	    )
2321	
2322	    # Cap academic results to avoid noise domination
2323	    academic_cap = min(len(academic_sorted), MAX_SOURCES_TO_ANALYZE)
2324	    academic_sorted = academic_sorted[:academic_cap]
2325	
2326	    # PL: Academic-first interleave (60% academic, 40% web).
2327	    # Previous: 60% web, 40% academic — correct when S2/OpenAlex was broken.
2328	    # Now Scholar + S2 parallel delivers real academic papers. Prioritize them.
2329	    merged: list[dict] = []
2330	    web_idx = 0
2331	    acad_idx = 0
2332	    seen_urls: set[str] = set()
2333	
2334	    while len(merged) < MAX_SOURCES_TO_ANALYZE * 2:
2335	        # Take 3 academic results first
2336	        for _ in range(3):
2337	            if acad_idx < len(academic_sorted):
2338	                url = academic_sorted[acad_idx].get("url", "")
2339	                if url not in seen_urls:
2340	                    merged.append(academic_sorted[acad_idx])
2341	                    seen_urls.add(url)
2342	                acad_idx += 1
2343	        # Take 2 web results
2344	        for _ in range(2):
2345	            if web_idx < len(web_sorted):
2346	                url = web_sorted[web_idx].get("url", "")
2347	                if url not in seen_urls:
2348	                    merged.append(web_sorted[web_idx])
2349	                    seen_urls.add(url)
2350	                web_idx += 1
2351	        # Stop when both pools exhausted
2352	        if web_idx >= len(web_sorted) and acad_idx >= len(academic_sorted):
2353	            break
2354	
2355	    logger.info(
2356	        "[polaris graph] PL: Academic-first merge: %d academic + %d web = %d total",
2357	        acad_idx, web_idx, len(merged),
2358	    )
2359	    return merged

2364	    query: str,
2365	) -> list[EvidencePiece]:
2366	    """IMP-2: Replace LLM-assigned relevance with embedding-based cosine similarity.
2367	
2368	    LLM-assigned scores cluster at midpoints (300/441 identical at 0.70).
2369	    Embedding similarity is deterministic and produces a true continuum.
2370	    Original LLM score is preserved as ``llm_relevance_score`` for debugging.
2371	
2372	    Graceful fallback: if embedding fails, keeps LLM scores unchanged.
2373	
2374	    FIX-F1: Handles Windows OSError errno 22 (Invalid argument) from long
2375	    cache paths by setting SENTENCE_TRANSFORMERS_HOME to a short path.

2378	        return evidence

2380	    def _do_embedding(retry_count: int = 0):
2381	        """Inner embedding logic with optional retry for FIX-F1."""
2382	        from src.utils.embedding_service import embed_text, embed_texts
2383	
2384	        # Embed the query once
2385	        query_vec = np.array(embed_text(query))
2386	
2387	        # Batch-embed all evidence statements
2388	        statements = [e.get("statement", "") for e in evidence]
2389	        statement_vecs = np.array(embed_texts(statements))
2390	
2391	        # Cosine similarity (embeddings are pre-normalized -> dot product)
2392	        similarities = statement_vecs @ query_vec
2393	        return similarities
2394	
2395	    try:
2396	        try:
2397	            similarities = _do_embedding()
2398	        except OSError as os_err:
2399	            # FIX-F1: Windows errno 22 (Invalid argument) from long cache paths
2400	            if os_err.errno == errno.EINVAL:
2401	                short_cache = os.getenv(
2402	                    "PG_ST_CACHE_PATH", r"C:\st_cache"
2403	                )
2404	                logger.warning(
2405	                    "[polaris graph] FIX-F1: OSError errno 22 (Windows path issue). "
2406	                    "Setting SENTENCE_TRANSFORMERS_HOME=%s and retrying once.",
2407	                    short_cache,
2408	                )
2409	                os.environ["SENTENCE_TRANSFORMERS_HOME"] = short_cache
2410	                # Force re-import / re-init by clearing singleton
2411	                try:
2412	                    from src.utils import embedding_service as _es_mod

2485	def _compute_quote_substance(quote: str) -> float:
2486	    """FIX-047-K7: Score quote substantiveness (0.0 to 1.0).
2487	
2488	    T047 audit root cause: PubMed URL reference was assigned GOLD because
2489	    the single-factor domain_authority * LLM_relevance had no secondary
2490	    validation of quote substance. This function scores the actual content
2491	    quality of the direct_quote.
2492	
2493	    Signals:
2494	    - Word count (< 5 words → 0.0, 5-10 → 0.3, 10-20 → 0.6, 20+ → 1.0)
2495	    - Contains numbers/data (concrete evidence)
2496	    - Contains proper sentences (not just fragments)
2497	    - NOT a URL, nav label, or abbreviation
2498	    """
2499	    if not quote:
2500	        return 0.0
2501	
2502	    words = quote.split()
2503	    word_count = len(words)
2504	
2505	    # Absolute veto: less than 5 words is never substantive
2506	    if word_count < 5:
2507	        return 0.0
2508	
2509	    # Word count score (0.0-0.4)
2510	    if word_count >= 20:
2511	        wc_score = 0.4
2512	    elif word_count >= 10:
2513	        wc_score = 0.3
2514	    elif word_count >= 7:
2515	        wc_score = 0.2
2516	    else:
2517	        wc_score = 0.1
2518	
2519	    # Contains numbers/measurements (0.0-0.2)
2520	    import re as _re
2521	    has_numbers = bool(_re.search(r"\d+\.?\d*", quote))
2522	    number_score = 0.2 if has_numbers else 0.0
2523	
2524	    # Contains sentence structure (has period, question, or is multi-clause) (0.0-0.2)
2525	    has_sentence = "." in quote or "?" in quote or ", " in quote
2526	    sentence_score = 0.2 if has_sentence else 0.0
2527	
2528	    # Contains unique type-token ratio (lexical diversity) (0.0-0.2)
2529	    unique_words = set(w.lower() for w in words if len(w) > 2)
2530	    ttr = len(unique_words) / max(1, word_count)
2531	    diversity_score = min(0.2, ttr * 0.3)
2532	
2533	    return min(1.0, wc_score + number_score + sentence_score + diversity_score)

2536	def _compute_freshness(ev: dict) -> float:
2537	    """FIX-048-K2: Compute freshness score from publication year.
2538	
2539	    Returns 0.0-1.0: current year = 1.0, loses 0.1 per year, min 0.0.
2540	    """
2541	    year = ev.get("year")
2542	    if not year:
2543	        return 0.3  # Unknown year — neutral default
2544	    try:
2545	        year = int(year)
2546	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
2547	        return 0.3
2548	    current_year = datetime.date.today().year
2549	    age = current_year - year
2550	    return max(0.0, min(1.0, 1.0 - age * 0.1))

3212	def _validate_extraction_claims(evidence: list[EvidencePiece]) -> list[EvidencePiece]:
3213	    """NRC-5: Post-extraction validation of evidence claims.
3214	
3215	    For each evidence piece:
3216	    1. Verify direct_quote is approximately found in source_content (fuzzy match).
3217	       If not found and source_content exists, mark quote_verified=False.
3218	    2. Detect claim inflation: if a single cross-reference claim has > 20
3219	       evidence_ids, flag as suspicious and cap at 10 most relevant.
3220	
3221	    Returns the evidence list with quote_verified flags updated.
3222	    """
3223	    max_claim_evidence = int(os.getenv("PG_MAX_EVIDENCE_PER_CLAIM", "20"))
3224	    validated = 0
3225	    unverified = 0
3226	
3227	    for ev in evidence:
3228	        quote = ev.get("direct_quote", "")
3229	        content = ev.get("source_content", "")
3230	
3231	        if not quote or not content:
3232	            continue
3233	
3234	        # Check if first 50 chars of quote appear in content (case-insensitive)
3235	        quote_prefix = quote[:50].lower().strip()
3236	        if len(quote_prefix) >= 10 and quote_prefix not in content.lower():
3237	            # Also try checking individual significant words (fuzzy match)
3238	            quote_words = {
3239	                w.lower() for w in quote[:100].split()
3240	                if len(w) > 4
3241	            }
3242	            if quote_words:
3243	                content_lower = content.lower()
3244	                matching_words = sum(1 for w in quote_words if w in content_lower)
3245	                match_ratio = matching_words / len(quote_words)
3246	                if match_ratio < 0.5:
3247	                    ev["quote_verified"] = False
3248	                    unverified += 1
3249	                else:
3250	                    ev["quote_verified"] = True
3251	                    validated += 1
3252	            else:
3253	                ev["quote_verified"] = True
3254	                validated += 1
3255	        else:
3256	            ev["quote_verified"] = True
3257	            validated += 1
3258	
3259	    if unverified > 0:
3260	        logger.warning(
3261	            "[polaris graph] NRC-5: %d/%d evidence pieces have unverified quotes "
3262	            "(direct_quote not found in source_content)",
3263	            unverified,
3264	            validated + unverified,
3265	        )
3266	    elif validated > 0:
3267	        logger.info(
3268	            "[polaris graph] NRC-5: All %d evidence quotes verified against source_content",
3269	            validated,
3270	        )
3271	
3272	    # NRC-5 Part 2: Claim inflation detection
3273	    # Group evidence by source_url. If a single source has > max_claim_evidence
3274	    # pieces, cap at the most relevant to prevent citation inflation from
3275	    # paywall-blocked or LLM-fabricated sources.
3276	    if max_claim_evidence > 0 and len(evidence) > max_claim_evidence:
3277	        source_groups: dict[str, list[int]] = {}
3278	        for idx, ev in enumerate(evidence):
3279	            src_url = ev.get("source_url", "")
3280	            if not src_url:
3281	                continue  # Skip evidence without source_url entirely
3282	            domain = urlparse(src_url).netloc or src_url
3283	            source_groups.setdefault(domain, []).append(idx)
3284	
3285	        capped_indices: set[int] = set()
3286	        inflation_capped = 0
3287	        for src_url, indices in source_groups.items():
3288	            if len(indices) > max_claim_evidence:
3289	                # Sort by relevance_score descending, keep top N
3290	                sorted_indices = sorted(
3291	                    indices,
3292	                    key=lambda i: evidence[i].get("relevance_score", 0.0),
3293	                    reverse=True,
3294	                )
3295	                keep = set(sorted_indices[:max_claim_evidence])
3296	                drop = set(sorted_indices[max_claim_evidence:])
3297	                capped_indices.update(drop)
3298	                inflation_capped += len(drop)
3299	                logger.info(
3300	                    "[polaris graph] NRC-5: Source %s had %d evidence pieces, "
3301	                    "capped to %d most relevant",
3302	                    src_url[:80], len(indices), max_claim_evidence,
3303	                )
3304	
3305	        if inflation_capped > 0:
3306	            evidence = [
3307	                ev for idx, ev in enumerate(evidence)
3308	                if idx not in capped_indices
3309	            ]
3310	            logger.info(
3311	                "[polaris graph] NRC-5: Claim inflation capping removed %d "
3312	                "evidence pieces (%d remaining)",
3313	                inflation_capped, len(evidence),
3314	            )
3315	
3316	    return evidence

3319	def _ground_quotes_verbatim(evidence: list[EvidencePiece]) -> list[EvidencePiece]:
3320	    """FIX-047I: Replace LLM-generated quotes with verbatim source text.
3321	
3322	    Implements the LangExtract deterministic quoting pattern:
3323	    - Uses the LLM's approximate quote as a search anchor
3324	    - Finds the best-matching substring in source_content
3325	    - Replaces the LLM quote with the verbatim source text
3326	
3327	    This eliminates the 3.2% embellishment rate where short LLM quotes
3328	    (1-3 words) were expanded into longer claims. After grounding, all
3329	    quotes are provably verbatim from the source.
3330	    """
3331	    grounded = 0
3332	    failed = 0
3333	
3334	    for ev in evidence:
3335	        quote = ev.get("direct_quote", "")
3336	        content = ev.get("source_content", "")
3337	
3338	        if not quote or not content or len(quote) < 10:
3339	            continue
3340	
3341	        content_lower = content.lower()
3342	        quote_lower = quote.lower().strip()
3343	
3344	        # Strategy 1: Exact substring match (most quotes)
3345	        idx = content_lower.find(quote_lower)
3346	        if idx >= 0:
3347	            # Replace with verbatim text (preserving original casing)
3348	            ev["direct_quote"] = content[idx:idx + len(quote)]
3349	            ev["quote_grounded"] = True
3350	            grounded += 1
3351	            continue
3352	
3353	        # Strategy 2: Progressive prefix match (handles LLM truncation)
3354	        found = False
3355	        for prefix_len in [80, 60, 40, 25]:
3356	            if prefix_len >= len(quote_lower):
3357	                continue
3358	            prefix = quote_lower[:prefix_len]
3359	            idx = content_lower.find(prefix)
3360	            if idx >= 0:
3361	                # Found prefix — extract verbatim text of similar length
3362	                end = min(len(content), idx + len(quote) + 50)
3363	                # Find sentence boundary near expected end
3364	                candidate = content[idx:end]
3365	                # Trim at sentence boundary (period, exclamation, question mark)
3366	                for punct in ['. ', '.\n', '! ', '? ']:
3367	                    punct_idx = candidate.find(punct, len(quote) - 20)
3368	                    if punct_idx > 0:
3369	                        candidate = candidate[:punct_idx + 1]
3370	                        break
3371	                else:
3372	                    # No sentence boundary found — take original quote length
3373	                    # FIX-059-D (H-07): Extend to next word boundary to avoid mid-word cuts
3374	                    end_pos = idx + len(quote)
3375	                    _space_pos = content.find(' ', end_pos)
3376	                    if _space_pos != -1 and _space_pos - end_pos < 50:
3377	                        end_pos = _space_pos
3378	                    candidate = content[idx:end_pos]
3379	
3380	                ev["direct_quote"] = candidate.strip()
3381	                ev["quote_grounded"] = True
3382	                grounded += 1
3383	                found = True
3384	                break
3385	
3386	        if not found:
3387	            # Strategy 3: Keyword density search (last resort)
3388	            words = [w.lower() for w in quote.split() if len(w) > 4]
3389	            if len(words) >= 3:
3390	                best_idx = -1
3391	                best_hits = 0
3392	                # Scan content in 200-char windows
3393	                for scan_idx in range(0, len(content_lower) - 200, 100):
3394	                    # FIX-059-D (H-08): Snap window start to word boundary
3395	                    _win_start = scan_idx
3396	                    if _win_start > 0:
3397	                        _sp = content_lower.rfind(' ', max(0, _win_start - 20), _win_start)
3398	                        if _sp != -1:
3399	                            _win_start = _sp + 1
3400	                    window = content_lower[_win_start:_win_start + 200]
3401	                    hits = sum(1 for w in words if w in window)
3402	                    if hits > best_hits:
3403	                        best_hits = hits
3404	                        best_idx = scan_idx
3405	
3406	                if best_hits >= len(words) * 0.6 and best_idx >= 0:
3407	                    # FIX-059-D (H-08): Snap extraction start to word boundary
3408	                    _ext_start = best_idx
3409	                    if _ext_start > 0:
3410	                        _sp2 = content.rfind(' ', max(0, _ext_start - 20), _ext_start)
3411	                        if _sp2 != -1:
3412	                            _ext_start = _sp2 + 1
3413	                    _ext_end = _ext_start + len(quote) + 30
3414	                    # FIX-059-D (H-07): Extend to word boundary on extraction end
3415	                    _sp3 = content.find(' ', _ext_end)
3416	                    if _sp3 != -1 and _sp3 - _ext_end < 50:
3417	                        _ext_end = _sp3
3418	                    verbatim = content[_ext_start:_ext_end].strip()
3419	                    # Trim to sentence boundary
3420	                    for punct in ['. ', '.\n']:
3421	                        punct_idx = verbatim.find(punct, len(quote) - 20)
3422	                        if punct_idx > 0:
3423	                            verbatim = verbatim[:punct_idx + 1]
3424	                            break
3425	                    ev["direct_quote"] = verbatim
3426	                    ev["quote_grounded"] = True
3427	                    grounded += 1
3428	                else:
3429	                    ev["quote_grounded"] = False
3430	                    failed += 1
3431	            else:
3432	                ev["quote_grounded"] = False
3433	                failed += 1
3434	
3435	    if grounded > 0 or failed > 0:
3436	        logger.info(
3437	            "[polaris graph] FIX-047I: Deterministic quoting: %d/%d quotes "
3438	            "grounded verbatim (%d could not be matched)",

3463	    semhash_threshold = float(os.getenv("PG_SEMHASH_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD", "0.85"))
3464	
3465	    if max_per_url <= 0 or len(evidence) <= 1:
3466	        return evidence
3467	
3468	    # Group by source URL
3469	    from collections import defaultdict
3470	    url_groups: dict[str, list[EvidencePiece]] = defaultdict(list)
3471	    for e in evidence:
3472	        url = e.get("source_url", "unknown")
3473	        url_groups[url].append(e)
3474	
3475	    # Try SemHash semantic dedup if enabled
3476	    if semhash_enabled:
3477	        result = _semhash_dedup_per_url(url_groups, semhash_threshold, max_per_url)
3478	        if result is not None:
3479	            return result
3480	
3481	    # Fallback: count-based cap
3482	    return _count_cap_per_url(url_groups, max_per_url, len(evidence))
3483	
3484	
3485	def _semhash_dedup_per_url(
3486	    url_groups: dict[str, list],
3487	    threshold: float,
3488	    max_per_url: int,
3489	) -> list[EvidencePiece] | None:
3490	    """Run SemHash self_deduplicate within each URL group.
3491	
3492	    Returns deduplicated evidence list, or None if SemHash unavailable
3493	    (caller should fall back to count cap).
3494	    """
3495	    try:
3496	        from semhash import SemHash
3497	    except ImportError:
3498	        logger.info(
3499	            "[polaris graph] FIX-048-K13: SemHash not installed — "

3500	            "falling back to count-based cap. Install with: pip install semhash"
3501	        )
3502	        return None
3503	
3504	    deduped: list[EvidencePiece] = []
3505	    total_removed = 0
3506	    groups_deduped = 0
3507	    t0 = time.time()
3508	
3509	    for url, pieces in url_groups.items():
3510	        if len(pieces) <= 1:
3511	            deduped.extend(pieces)
3512	            continue
3513	
3514	        # Extract statements for SemHash
3515	        statements = [p.get("statement", "") for p in pieces]
3516	
3517	        try:
3518	            semhash_index = SemHash.from_records(records=statements)
3519	            result = semhash_index.self_deduplicate(threshold=threshold)
3520	
3521	            # Map selected records back to evidence pieces.
3522	            # SemHash result.selected contains the deduplicated text records.
3523	            selected_stmts = list(result.selected)
3524	            selected_set = set(selected_stmts)
3525	            kept = []
3526	            for i, piece in enumerate(pieces):
3527	                if statements[i] in selected_set:
3528	                    kept.append(piece)
3529	                    selected_set.discard(statements[i])
3530	
3531	            # Apply count cap on top of semantic dedup
3532	            if len(kept) > max_per_url:
3533	                kept.sort(
3534	                    key=lambda e: (
3535	                        e.get("relevance_score", 0.0),
3536	                        len(e.get("statement", "")),
3537	                    ),
3538	                    reverse=True,
3539	                )
3540	                kept = kept[:max_per_url]
3541	
3542	            removed_count = len(pieces) - len(kept)
3543	            total_removed += removed_count
3544	            deduped.extend(kept)
3545	            if removed_count > 0:
3546	                groups_deduped += 1
3547	
3548	        except Exception as exc:
3549	            logger.debug(
3550	                "[polaris graph] FIX-048-K13: SemHash failed for URL %s (%d pieces): %s — "
3551	                "falling back to count cap for this group",
3552	                url[:60], len(pieces), str(exc)[:100],
3553	            )
3554	            # Count-cap fallback for this single group
3555	            if len(pieces) > max_per_url:
3556	                sorted_pieces = sorted(
3557	                    pieces,
3558	                    key=lambda e: (
3559	                        e.get("relevance_score", 0.0),
3560	                        len(e.get("statement", "")),
3561	                    ),
3562	                    reverse=True,
3563	                )
3564	                deduped.extend(sorted_pieces[:max_per_url])
3565	                total_removed += len(pieces) - max_per_url
3566	                groups_deduped += 1
3567	            else:
3568	                deduped.extend(pieces)
3569	
3570	    elapsed = time.time() - t0
3571	    total_input = sum(len(p) for p in url_groups.values())
3572	    if total_removed > 0:
3573	        logger.info(
3574	            "[polaris graph] FIX-048-K13: SemHash dedup removed %d/%d evidence "
3575	            "pieces (%d URL groups deduped, threshold=%.2f, %.1fs)",
3576	            total_removed, total_input, groups_deduped, threshold, elapsed,
3577	        )
3578	    else:
3579	        logger.info(
3580	            "[polaris graph] FIX-048-K13: SemHash dedup kept all %d evidence "
3581	            "(no semantic duplicates above threshold=%.2f, %.1fs)",
3582	            total_input, threshold, elapsed,
3583	        )
3584	
3585	    return deduped
3586	
3587	
3588	def _count_cap_per_url(
3589	    url_groups: dict[str, list],
3590	    max_per_url: int,
3591	    total_evidence: int,
3592	) -> list[EvidencePiece]:
3593	    """Fallback: simple count cap per URL (pre-FIX-048 behavior)."""
3594	    capped: list[EvidencePiece] = []
3595	    total_removed = 0
3596	    for url, pieces in url_groups.items():
3597	        if len(pieces) <= max_per_url:
3598	            capped.extend(pieces)
3599	        else:
3600	            sorted_pieces = sorted(
3601	                pieces,
3602	                key=lambda e: (
3603	                    e.get("relevance_score", 0.0),
3604	                    len(e.get("statement", "")),
3605	                ),
3606	                reverse=True,
3607	            )
3608	            capped.extend(sorted_pieces[:max_per_url])
3609	            total_removed += len(pieces) - max_per_url
3610	
3611	    if total_removed > 0:
3612	        logger.info(
3613	            "[polaris graph] FIX-047-K13: Per-URL count cap (%d) removed %d/%d "
3614	            "evidence pieces (%d URLs capped)",
3615	            max_per_url, total_removed, total_evidence,
3616	            sum(1 for pieces in url_groups.values() if len(pieces) > max_per_url),
3617	        )
3618	
3619	    return capped
3620	
3621	
3622	def _deduplicate_evidence(evidence: list[EvidencePiece]) -> list[EvidencePiece]:
3623	    """Deduplicate evidence using MinHash from ContentDeduplicator.
3624	
3625	    Same fact from different sources creates duplicate evidence.
3626	    MinHash efficiently detects near-duplicate statements.
3627	    Controlled by PG_EVIDENCE_DEDUP_ENABLED env var.
3628	    """
3629	    if not PG_EVIDENCE_DEDUP_ENABLED or len(evidence) <= 1:
3630	        return evidence
3631	
3632	    try:
3633	        from src.utils.content_deduplicator import ContentDeduplicator
3634	
3635	        dedup = ContentDeduplicator()
3636	        items = [
3637	            {"content": e.get("statement", ""), **e}
3638	            for e in evidence
3639	        ]
3640	        result = dedup.deduplicate(items, content_key="content")
3641	
3642	        if result.unique_count < len(evidence):
3643	            logger.info(
3644	                "[polaris graph] Evidence dedup: %d -> %d unique "
3645	                "(%d exact, %d near-duplicates removed)",
3646	                len(evidence),
3647	                result.unique_count,
3648	                result.exact_duplicates,
3649	                result.near_duplicates,
3650	            )
3651	
3652	        # WAVE-3.3: Dedup detail trace
3653	        tracer = get_tracer()
3654	        if tracer:
3655	            dup_pairs = []
3656	            if hasattr(result, "duplicates"):
3657	                for d in result.duplicates:
3658	                    dup_pairs.append({
3659	                        "original_idx": getattr(d, "original_index", 0),
3660	                        "duplicate_idx": getattr(d, "duplicate_index", 0),
3661	                        "similarity": round(getattr(d, "similarity", 0), 3),
3662	                        "type": getattr(d, "duplicate_type", ""),
3663	                    })
3664	            tracer.evidence("analyze", "dedup_detail", len(evidence),
3665	                before_count=len(evidence),
3666	                after_count=result.unique_count,
3667	                exact_removed=result.exact_duplicates,
3668	                near_removed=result.near_duplicates,
3669	                minhash_pairs=dup_pairs[:200])
3670	
3671	        # Convert back to EvidencePiece format (remove the extra "content" key)
3672	        deduped: list[EvidencePiece] = []
3673	        for item in result.unique_items:
3674	            # Remove the "content" key we added for dedup
3675	            item.pop("content", None)
3676	            deduped.append(item)
3677	
3678	        return deduped
3679	
3680	    except ImportError:
3681	        logger.warning(
3682	            "[polaris graph] ContentDeduplicator not available — skipping dedup"
3683	        )
3684	        return evidence
3685	    except Exception as exc:
3686	        logger.warning(
3687	            "[polaris graph] Evidence dedup failed: %s — skipping",
3688	            str(exc)[:200],
3689	        )
3690	        return evidence
3691	
3692	
3693	def _make_evidence_id(url: str, statement: str) -> str:
3694	    """Generate deterministic evidence ID."""
3695	    raw = f"{url}:{statement}"
3696	    return "ev_" + hashlib.sha256(raw.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]
