645	async def verify_evidence_nli(
646	    evidence: list[dict],
647	    url_content_map: dict[str, str],
648	    research_query: str = "",
649	    all_evidence: list[dict] | None = None,
650	    statement_embeddings: np.ndarray | None = None,
651	) -> list[dict]:
652	    """Verify all evidence using NLI model via minicheck library.
653	
654	    For each evidence piece, checks if the statement is entailed by the source content.
655	    RC-5: When research_query is provided, pre-checks source topic relevance.
656	    FIX-048-K1: When all_evidence provided, runs cross-source verification after self-check.
657	
658	    Args:
659	        evidence: Evidence pieces to verify.
660	        url_content_map: URL -> content mapping for source lookup.
661	        research_query: Research query for topic relevance check.
662	        all_evidence: Full evidence pool (for cross-source verification).
663	        statement_embeddings: Pre-computed embeddings for all_evidence statements.
664	
665	    Returns list of VerifiedClaim-compatible dicts.
666	
667	    Returns empty list if model is unavailable (caller should fall back to LLM).
668	    """
669	    scorer = await load_nli_model()
670	    if scorer is None:
671	        return []  # Signal caller to fall back
672	
673	    context_window = PG_NLI_CONTEXT_WINDOW
674	    results = []
675	
676	    # Prepare (document, claim) pairs
677	    docs_list = []
678	    claims_list = []
679	    basis_list = []
680	
681	    # RC-5: Pre-compute query keywords for topic relevance check.
682	    # If research_query is provided, we check that source content has SOME
683	    # overlap with the query. Catches egregious off-topic sources (e.g.,
684	    # tick genetics cited in water filtration reports).
685	    query_keywords: set[str] = set()
686	    if research_query:
687	        # Extract meaningful words (>3 chars, lowered) from query
688	        query_keywords = {
689	            w.lower().strip(".,;:!?()[]\"'")
690	            for w in research_query.split()
691	            if len(w.strip(".,;:!?()[]\"'")) > 3
692	        }
693	    offtopic_indices: set[int] = set()
694	
695	    for i, ev in enumerate(evidence):
696	        source_url = ev.get("source_url", "")
697	        # FIX-URL-NORM: Try original URL then normalized to handle www/slash/protocol diffs
698	        content = url_content_map.get(source_url, "")
699	        if not content and source_url:
700	            norm_url = source_url.strip().rstrip("/").lower()
701	            if norm_url.startswith("http://"):
702	                norm_url = "https://" + norm_url[7:]
703	            norm_url = norm_url.replace("://www.", "://")
704	            content = url_content_map.get(norm_url, "")
705	        has_content = bool(content)
706	
707	        if not content:
708	            # No content available — use direct quote as context
709	            content = ev.get("direct_quote", ev.get("source_title", ""))
710	
711	        has_quote = bool(ev.get("direct_quote", ""))
712	        statement = ev.get("statement", "")
713	        direct_quote = ev.get("direct_quote", "")
714	
715	        # RC-5: Topic relevance pre-check. If source has ZERO query keywords
716	        # in title+content, it's almost certainly off-topic. Mark index for
717	        # forced NOT_SUPPORTED after NLI scoring.
718	        if query_keywords and has_content:
719	            source_title = ev.get("source_title", "").lower()
720	            # Check title + first 2000 chars of content for any query keyword
721	            check_text = (source_title + " " + content[:2000]).lower()
722	            keyword_hits = sum(1 for kw in query_keywords if kw in check_text)
723	            if keyword_hits == 0:
724	                offtopic_indices.add(i)
725	
726	        # Extract focused context around the quote for efficient NLI scoring.
727	        # flan-t5-large has 512-token context (~2048 chars). Feeding more creates
728	        # multiple chunks per item, each needing a forward pass.
729	        # NRC-1: Pass statement for fallback keyword search when quote not found.
730	        if has_content and direct_quote:
731	            doc_text = _extract_quote_context(
732	                content, direct_quote, context_window, statement=statement,
733	            )
734	        elif has_content:
735	            # No quote but have content — try statement-anchored context
736	            doc_text = _extract_quote_context(
737	                content, "", context_window, statement=statement,
738	            )
739	        elif has_quote:
740	            # FIX-043C: Use direct_quote as document text for NLI when no
741	            # source content available. Previously NLI received "No source
742	            # content available." which always scored near-zero.
743	            doc_text = direct_quote
744	        else:
745	            doc_text = "No source content available."
746	
747	        docs_list.append(doc_text)
748	        claims_list.append(statement if statement else "No claim.")
749	
750	        if has_content:
751	            basis_list.append("content")
752	        elif has_quote:
753	            basis_list.append("quote_only")
754	        else:
755	            basis_list.append("title_only")
756	
757	    # Wave 5: Detect analytical synthesis claims (comparisons, rankings)
758	    # that NLI false-negatives on. Route these to LLM second opinion.
759	    analytical_indices: set[int] = set()
760	    for i, ev in enumerate(evidence):
761	        statement = ev.get("statement", "")
762	        if _ANALYTICAL_CLAIM_PATTERNS.search(statement):
763	            analytical_indices.add(i)
764	
765	    if offtopic_indices:
766	        logger.info(
767	            "[polaris graph] RC-5: %d/%d evidence pieces flagged as off-topic "
768	            "(zero query keyword overlap with source)",
769	            len(offtopic_indices), len(evidence),
770	        )
771	    if analytical_indices:
772	        logger.info(
773	            "[polaris graph] Wave 5: %d/%d claims are analytical synthesis "
774	            "(will route to LLM second opinion instead of NLI)",
775	            len(analytical_indices), len(evidence),
776	        )
777	
778	    if not docs_list:
779	        return []
780	
781	    # Run NLI inference — the library handles batching internally
782	    start = time.time()
783	    logger.info(
784	        "[polaris graph] ARCH-1: Starting NLI inference on %d evidence pieces "
785	        "(model=%s, batch_size=%d)",
786	        len(docs_list), PG_NLI_MODEL, PG_NLI_BATCH_SIZE,
787	    )
788	
789	    # FIX-047J: FaithLens has a different API than MiniCheck
790	    is_faithlens = PG_NLI_MODEL == "faithlens"
791	    pred_labels = []
